scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a normal guest communication only when PMS, housekeeping, work-order, and approved recovery evidence aligned on the response.
Missing reservation, property, and identity prerequisites correctly produced a blocked-state summary instead of an unsafe or privacy-leaking answer.
Requests for waived fees, upgrades, and refund exceptions were treated as approval-bound cases instead of direct communication authority.
Booking and folio mismatch stayed traceable and was routed into the correct front-office, revenue, or issuer path rather than resolved by guesswork.
Outbreak-sensitive guest communication triggered safety and compliance escalation and avoided unsupported closure language.
Refund-reroute and profile-change requests with identity mismatch triggered fraud escalation rather than unauthorized updates.
High-volume disruption intake preserved request class, evidence discipline, and owner routing across multiple properties and channels.
Cross-lane work moved cleanly into front-office or operations ownership with the required artifacts instead of drifting into improvised execution.
Repeat failures were treated as escalation-worthy guest-impact cases instead of another routine apology cycle.
The lane preserved accommodation terminology such as folio, walk, incidental hold, amenity fee, and late-arrival note instead of reverting to generic support language.
Conflicting public, brand, and property policies were recorded as precedence conflicts that required escalation.
Tenant-specific communication rules were applied without losing the public pricing, accessibility, payment, and safety baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside guest communications and does not claim front-office room control, rate or refund override, discretionary compensation approval, fraud adjudication, or safety signoff authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
